description:
Our k8s manifest repo at /home/user/manifests uses a symlink structure for environment overlays — base configs in `base/`, then `envs/staging/` and `envs/prod/` symlink to pieces of base and override what they need. Was working fine until someone reorganized the base directory last week.

Now `kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f envs/staging/` chokes — something about invalid YAML or missing files, I forget the exact message. Prod overlay is probably broken too but staging is the one I need fixed first. The apply should succeed without touching the base directory structure (that reorg is intentional and staying).
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/manifests/ contains:
    - base/
        - services/
            - api-deployment.yaml (valid k8s Deployment)
            - api-service.yaml (valid k8s Service)
        - config/
            - configmap.yaml (valid ConfigMap)
    - envs/
        - staging/
            - deployment.yaml -> ../../base/api-deployment.yaml (VALID symlink, works)
            - service.yaml -> ../../base/services/api-service.yaml (BROKEN symlink — base/services/ exists but symlink points to old path ../../base/api-service.yaml which doesn't exist)
            - configmap.yaml -> ../../base/configmaps/configmap.yaml (BROKEN symlink — directory was renamed from configmaps/ to config/)
            - staging-ingress.yaml (real file, valid Ingress)
        - prod/
            - deployment.yaml -> ../../base/api-deployment.yaml (BROKEN — same issue, points to ../../base/services/api-deployment.yaml but file is at ../../base/services/api-deployment.yaml... wait, let me fix this)
            
Let me redo this more clearly:

- /home/user/manifests/base/services/api-deployment.yaml — valid Deployment manifest
- /home/user/manifests/base/services/api-service.yaml — valid Service manifest  
- /home/user/manifests/base/config/configmap.yaml — valid ConfigMap manifest

- /home/user/manifests/envs/staging/deployment.yaml -> ../../base/services/api-deployment.yaml (VALID, works)
- /home/user/manifests/envs/staging/service.yaml -> ../../base/api-service.yaml (BROKEN — old path before services/ subdir was created; actual file is at base/services/api-service.yaml)
- /home/user/manifests/envs/staging/configmap.yaml -> ../../base/configmaps/configmap.yaml (BROKEN — directory was renamed from configmaps/ to config/)
- /home/user/manifests/envs/staging/staging-ingress.yaml — real file, valid Ingress

- /home/user/manifests/envs/prod/deployment.yaml -> ../../base/services/api-deployment.yaml (VALID)
- /home/user/manifests/envs/prod/service.yaml -> ../../base/api-service.yaml (BROKEN — same issue as staging)
- /home/user/manifests/envs/prod/configmap.yaml -> ../../base/configmaps/configmap.yaml (BROKEN — same issue as staging)
- /home/user/manifests/envs/prod/prod-ingress.yaml — real file, valid Ingress

- kubectl is installed and works. Running `kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f envs/staging/` currently fails because it tries to read the broken symlinks and gets "no such file or directory" or invalid content.
- All directories and files under /home/user/manifests are writable by the agent.
- The base/ directory structure is intentional and must not be changed.

Expected final state:
- `cd /home/user/manifests && kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f envs/staging/` exits 0 and outputs the dry-run results for all four resources (Deployment, Service, ConfigMap, Ingress).
- All symlinks in envs/staging/ point to valid targets under base/.
- The actual manifest content is still sourced from base/ (symlinks fixed, not replaced with copies of the files).

Invariants:
- /home/user/manifests/base/ directory structure unchanged (no moving files back to old locations).
- All YAML files in base/ byte-identical to initial state.
- envs/staging/staging-ingress.yaml byte-identical to initial state.
- envs/prod/ may or may not be fixed — not required, but must not be made worse.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- `file envs/staging/service.yaml envs/staging/configmap.yaml` must report "symbolic link" for both (not replaced with regular files).
- `readlink envs/staging/service.yaml` must contain "base/services/api-service.yaml" as a substring.
- `readlink envs/staging/configmap.yaml` must contain "base/config/configmap.yaml" as a substring.
name: task_000000_2884808f
